from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes
    def clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip().strip('"')

    # Mapping of predicates to natural language templates
    templates = {
        "author": lambda s, o: f"{s} was written by {o}",
        "publisher": lambda s, o: f"{s} was published by {o}",
        "editor": lambda s, o: f"{s} was edited by {o}",
        "founder": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded by {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by {o}",
        "president": lambda s, o: f"The president of {s} is {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"The headquarters of {s} is located in {o}",
        "parentCompany": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a subsidiary of {o}",
        "isbnNumber": lambda s, o: f"The ISBN of {s} is {o}",
        "issnNumber": lambda s, o: f"The ISSN of {s} is {o}",
        "eissnNumber": lambda s, o: f"The e‑ISSN of {s} is {o}",
        "LCCN_number": lambda s, o: f"The LCCN of {s} is {o}",
        "oclcNumber": lambda s, o: f"The OCLC number of {s} is {o}",
        "mediaType": lambda s, o: f"The media type of {s} is {o}",
        "numberOfPages": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o} pages",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{s} is written in {o}",
        "genre": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the {o} genre",
        "literaryGenre": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the {o} genre",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is from {o}",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in the state of {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the region of {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda s, o: f"The largest city in {s} is {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "family": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the {o} family",
        "genus": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the genus {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} is associated with the ethnic group {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "nickname": lambda s, o: f"{s} is nicknamed \"{o}\"",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "residence": lambda s, o: f"{s} resides in {o}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{s} has nationality {o}",
        "followedBy": lambda s, o: f"{s} is followed by {o}",
        "precededBy": lambda s, o: f"{s} is preceded by {o}",
        "releaseDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was released on {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "firstPublicationYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was first published in {o}",
        # Additional predicates not covered previously
        "codenCode": lambda s, o: f"The CODEN of {s} is {o}",
        "abbreviation": lambda s, o: f"The abbreviation of {s} is \"{o}\"",
        "academicDiscipline": lambda s, o: f"The academic discipline of {s} is {o}",
        "affiliation": lambda s, o: f"The affiliation of {s} is {o}",
    }

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        pred = t.predicate
        # Normalize predicate name (remove spaces, keep case for mapping keys)
        norm = pred[0].lower() + pred[1:] if pred else pred
        # Try direct match, otherwise camel‑case split and lower‑case join
        template = templates.get(pred) or templates.get(norm)
        if not template:
            # fallback: split camel case and join with spaces
            import re
            words = re.sub(r'(?<!^)(?=[A-Z])', ' ', pred).replace('_', ' ').lower()
            clause = f"{t.subject} {words} {clean(t.object)}"
        else:
            clause = template(t.subject, clean(t.object))
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Build a single fluent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
